process_name: Lepton-flavor-violating Higgs decay h -> e tau
implementation_difficulty_reason: The direct experimental limit is simple to catalog, but production-grade RS integration needs a new charged-lepton Higgs-Yukawa misalignment calculation, a convention for Y_e_tau/Y_tau_e and their relation to the branching ratio, and a Higgs production/total-width interpretation. The current code only has diagonal charged-lepton Yukawas and a mu -> e gamma dipole filter.

evidence: Targeted grep for h -> e tau / H -> e tau / etau / Y_e_tau / Y_tau_e across the required code directories returned no hits.
The existing charged-lepton LFV implementation is mu -> e gamma only: flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:1 and flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75.
The scan applies the mu -> e gamma LFV filter at scanParams/scan.py:523 and scanParams/scan.py:524; no h -> e tau branch or Higgs LFV limit is present.
Charged-lepton Yukawa support is a diagonal mass-relation helper at yukawa/charged_lepton.py:1 and yukawa/charged_lepton.py:36, not a Higgs LFV observable.
grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|H.?->|higgs|Higgs|LFV|lepton.?flavou?r|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "h\\s*[-=]>\\s*e\\s*tau|H\\s*[-=]>\\s*e\\s*tau|Higgs.*e\\s*tau|LFV.*Higgs|Higgs.*LFV|Y_e_tau|Y_tau_e|Yetau|etau" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(h\to e^\pm\tau^\mp)\).
This entry catalogs the lepton-flavor-violating Higgs decay \(h\to e\tau\)
in the top/Higgs/electroweak family.  It covers direct ATLAS and CMS searches
for a neutral Higgs boson decaying to an electron and an oppositely charged
tau lepton.
sections.pdg_prose:
The source-of-truth values are in the \texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent} block of
\texttt{T019.yaml}.  The PDG 2025 Higgs review summarizes the full
\(\sqrt{s}=13~\mathrm{TeV}\) Run-2 direct limits on LFV Higgs decays
(\texttt{PDG2025HiggsLFVReview}).  For \(h\to e\tau\), ATLAS gives
\[
  \mathcal{B}(h\to e\tau) < 0.20\% \quad (0.12\%\ \mathrm{expected})
\]
at 95\% CL using \(138~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\)
(\texttt{ATLAS2023HiggsLFVETau}; \texttt{PDG2025:T019:atlas\_run2};
\texttt{ATLAS2023:T019:dataset}).  CMS gives
\[
  \mathcal{B}(h\to e\tau) < 0.22\% \quad (0.16\%\ \mathrm{expected})
\]
at 95\% CL using \(137~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\)
(\texttt{CMS2021HiggsLFVETau}; \texttt{PDG2025:T019:cms\_run2};
\texttt{CMS2021:T019:dataset}).  The strongest observed
single-experiment headline value in the local snapshots is therefore the
ATLAS \(2.0\times 10^{-3}\) branching-fraction limit
(\texttt{ATLAS2023:T019:etau\_limit}).
sections.relevance:
In warped or partially composite lepton sectors, flavor-dependent localization
can explain charged-lepton masses while leaving the physical Higgs Yukawa
matrix misaligned with the mass matrix.  The \(h\to e\tau\) limit directly
tests the \(e\)-\(\tau\) off-diagonal charged-lepton Yukawa entries.  It is
complementary to dipole constraints such as \(\mu\to e\gamma\), but it is
not part of the current quark \(\Delta F=2\) scan lane.
sections.post_2008:
The baseline arXiv:0804.1954 predates direct LHC Higgs LFV searches
(\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008WarpedFlavor}).  Harnik, Kopp, and Zupan
later emphasized that \(h\to\tau e\) could be a discovery channel and that
branching fractions of order \(10\%\) were still possible in generic
LFV-Higgs EFT settings after low-energy constraints
(\texttt{HarnikKoppZupan2012FlavorViolatingHiggs}).  ATLAS performed an early
\(13~\mathrm{TeV}\) search with \(36.1~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\), setting
\(\mathcal{B}(H\to e\tau)<0.47\%\)
(\texttt{ATLAS2019HiggsLFVETau}).  The full Run-2 CMS and ATLAS analyses then
pushed the direct e-tau limit to the few-\(10^{-3}\) level, with no significant
excess (\texttt{CMS2021HiggsLFVETau}; \texttt{ATLAS2023HiggsLFVETau}).
sections.validity:
Experimentally this is a direct upper limit on a Standard-Model-forbidden
decay.  A literal branching-ratio cut assumes the Higgs production mixture
and total width used by the collaborations.  A model with altered Higgs
production rates, new invisible width, or correlated LFV tau decays would need
a recast.  For the catalog, the limit is robust as an experimental observable
but model-dependent as an RS acceptance criterion.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
Coverage is \textbf{NO}.  The required greps and a targeted search for
\texttt{h->e tau}, \texttt{H->e tau}, \texttt{etau}, \texttt{Y\_e\_tau}, and
\texttt{Y\_tau\_e} across \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/} found no observable implementation.  The related LFV backend
is specifically \(\mu\to e\gamma\)
(\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:1};
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}), and the scan applies that
filter at \texttt{scanParams/scan.py:523}--\texttt{scanParams/scan.py:524}.
The charged-lepton Yukawa helper is diagonal
(\texttt{yukawa/charged\_lepton.py:1}; \texttt{yukawa/charged\_lepton.py:36}).
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
Production integration is \textbf{HIGH}.  The external limit is simple to
catalog, but a faithful RS constraint needs a new charged-lepton Higgs-Yukawa
misalignment calculation, a convention for \(Y_{e\tau}\) and \(Y_{\tau e}\),
and a Higgs-rate/width interpretation before scan points can be accepted or
rejected.
